Catastrophic blazes claimed lives, homes and jobs last year in California, Canada, Europe and South Korea. But the 335m hectares burned was the second-lowest since 2002, the review found, largely owing to the expansion of African farms that have fragmented landscapes and hampered the spread of large savannah fires.

A powerful tropical storm is forecast to track near Okinawa, Japan, on Monday before moving towards the south-east of the country. Typhoon Jangmi (also known as Typhoon No 6) has formed within the monsoonal gyre over the Philippine Sea.

A monsoonal gyre is a large, slow-rotating weather system that spawns typhoons through smaller vortices formed within it. This flow can intensify storms. Such typhoons are typically characterised by broad areas of low pressure and extensive wind fields, often without a distinct eye.

events.title: Far-right praise for shah’s secret police puts Reza Pahlavi on the spot
events.summary:
Son of deposed shah forced to distance himself from once-dreaded Savak as some of his ‘fascistic’ supporters glorify it

For decades, the Savak was seen as the most hated symbol of repression that kept Iran’s last shah, Mohammad Reza Pahlavi, in power – and a main driving force behind the revolutionary fervor that toppled him in 1979.

Now the deposed monarch’s son, Reza Pahlavi, has been forced to distance himself from the once-dreaded security agency after some of his most vociferous supporters glorified it as the defining emblem in their drive to install him on the throne in a royal restoration.

Continue reading...

events.title: Democrats who marched in New York’s Israel Day parade decry attendance of far-right minister Smotrich
events.summary:
Politicians’ statements reflect difficulty facing pro-Israel Democrats as voter support for country falls

Several prominent New York Democrats who participated in the city’s annual Israel Day parade on Sunday have condemned the participation of Bezalel Smotrich, the far-right Israeli finance minister and a leading figure in the Israeli settler movement, in the event.

Smotrich was among several Israeli lawmakers and cabinet officials who marched in the parade on Sunday. His appearance marked his first trip to the US in more than a year, and came less than month after he said the international criminal court (ICC) was seeking an arrest warrant against him.

Continue reading...

events.title: Why Benjamin Netanyahu poses an obstacle to US and Iran peace deal
events.summary:
Prime minister under pressure to show his campaigns against Hamas, Hezbollah and Iran have brought results as he faces elections with his political survival at risk

If there is to be a peace deal between the United States and Iran, it will have to go through a familiar obstacle: Benjamin Netanyahu. Israel’s military operations in Lebanon have become a sticking point in the talks for a potential opening of the strait of Hormuz – once again testing the volatile alliance between Donald Trump and Netanyahu.

This time, the Israeli prime minister is under exceptional pressure to show that his campaigns against Hamas, Hezbollah and Iran have brought results as he faces elections with his political survival at risk.

Continue reading...

events.title: Israel strikes southern Lebanon despite Trump’s effort to shore up ceasefire
events.summary:
Warplanes carry out dozens of airstrikes and Israeli army issues evacuation warning for city of Nabatiyeh

Israeli warplanes have launched dozens of strikes across southern Lebanon despite a new agreement supposedly brokered by Donald Trump aiming to bolster the tattered ceasefire in Lebanon.

The US president said on Monday that he had stopped an imminent Israeli strike on Beirut and that he had spoken to Israel’s prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, and representatives of Hezbollah and both agreed that “all shooting will stop”.

Continue reading...

events.title: Eighteen people killed in Afghanistan truck crash, including 10 children
events.summary:
Truck was carrying Afghan families returning Pakistan when it overturned, official says

A truck overturned in eastern Afghanistan on Saturday, killing 18 people on board including 10 children, a provincial official told Agence France-Presse.

Deadly traffic crashes are common in Afghanistan, due in part to poor roads after decades of conflict, dangerous driving and a lack of regulation.

Continue reading...

events.title: Exam fail: Indian students complain en masse about marking errors in key final exams
events.summary:
New digital marking system is aimed at reducing human errors but many students say it has resulted in wrong grades

A national outcry has erupted in India after more than 400,000 students requested copies of their answer sheets amid mounting complaints of errors in the marking of the country’s most important school-leaving examinations.

Within days of the grade 12 exam results being issued, students began reporting marking discrepancies they linked to a new digital marking system.

Continue reading...

events.title: Blond Bangladeshi buffalo nicknamed ‘Donald Trump’ saved from Eid sacrifice
events.summary:
Rare albino buffalo spared due to security concerns over unusual level of public interest in 700kg animal

A rare albino buffalo in Bangladesh nicknamed “Donald Trump” for its distinctive blond tuft has been spared from Eid al-Adha sacrifice after a last-minute government intervention, according to a home ministry official.

The nearly 700kg (1,543lb) animal had already been sold for ritual slaughter when authorities stepped in, citing security concerns after a surge of public interest before Thursday’s festival.

Continue reading...

events.title: Indians protest over ‘forever chemicals’ after relocation of scandal-hit Italian factory
events.summary:
Lack of Pfas regulations raised in parliament after Guardian revealed former Miteni plant bought by Indian company

Protests over the production of cancer-linked Pfas chemicals have spread across India, after an investigation revealed that an Italian factory shut down due to an environmental scandal was bought by an Indian company and partly rebuilt.

At the end of last year, the Guardian revealed that the former Miteni plant in Vicenza had been acquired by the Indian company Laxmi Organic Industries. The factory produced Pfas and was shut down in 2018 after being linked to one of Italy’s worst environmental contamination scandals.

Continue reading...

events.title: Starmer urged to intervene in ‘rigged’ Indian prosecution of British human rights activist
events.summary:
Senior lawyers call on prime minister to request Indian prosecutors drop charges that would breach double jeopardy rule

Four senior lawyers, including the former attorney general Dominic Grieve, have written to Keir Starmer urging him to request that Indian prosecutors drop charges against the British national Jagtar Singh Johal on the basis that continued prosecution would be in manifest breach of the double jeopardy rule which prevents someone being tried twice for the same offence.

Johal has been held in an Indian jail for eight years, and in March last year was acquitted of the terrorist charges laid against him in a court in Punjab. The court found the prosecutors had “miserably failed” to present any reliable evidence, despite having had seven years to do so.

Continue reading...

events.title: Thousands sign petition against cuts to tech support for disabled students in England
events.summary:
DfE plans to withdraw funding for assistive software, saying it is now rarely needed due to ‘widely available free tools’

Disability campaigners have called on the government to halt plans to cut funding for specialist tech support for tens of thousands of disabled students in England.

Almost 10,000 people have signed a petition opposing Department for Education (DfE) proposals to withdraw funding for specialist assistive software, currently available as part of the Disabled Students’ Allowance (DSA).

Continue reading...

events.title: Farage exploiting Henry Nowak’s murder against wishes of his family, says Starmer – UK politics live
events.summary:
Reform leader jeered in parliament as he is urged to condemn violence

Zia Yusuf, the Reform UK home affairs spokesperson, told Sky News this morning that he thought the treatment of Henry Nowak did justify his party’s claim that two-tier policing operates in the country. He said:

Having watched that footage [of Nowak’s arrest] … it’s hard to escape the conclusion that it is a demonstrable example of structural two-tier policing that is embedded in Hampshire police force and forces across the country.

People can go to Hampshire police’s website and read their race action plan that was brought about under a Tory government.

We understand and appreciate as police officers that we are accountable for our actions. What we ask, however, is that those actions are judged through fair and transparent processes. In this case, that process is already underway with the IOPC conducting their independent investigation.

What we, as a society, cannot accept is the violent scenes we saw in Southampton last night.

I know that since the release of the body-worn video footage from the night of Henry Nowak’s murder, there is a desire for answers and accountability but that must be done in the right way and not used as an excuse to threaten and intimidate my officers and bring violence to our streets causing fear and harm to those living and working in Hampshire and the Isle of Wight.

Continue reading...

events.title: UK government to pay £1.3bn to help fund Universal Studios theme park in Bedfordshire
events.summary:
Chancellor hails deal saying it will create tens of thousands of jobs in the construction, hospitality, creative and technology sectors

  • Business live – latest update

British taxpayers will provide £1.3bn in funding to help Hollywood studio giant Universal build its first theme park in Europe.

Comcast, the US media company that owns NBC Universal and Sky, had been considering a number of countries in which to build its first European theme park.

Continue reading...

events.title: Three Royal Navy members killed in Devon helicopter crash
events.summary:
Ministry of Defence confirms deaths of three crew members after crash near Okehampton during training exercise

Three members of the Royal Navy have died in a helicopter crash in Devon during a training exercise, the Ministry of Defence (MoD) has said.

Emergency services were called to the scene of the incident at Sourton Down, near Okehampton, around 4am on Wednesday.

Continue reading...

events.title: Eleven officers and police dog injured in ‘terrifying’ violence in Southampton
events.summary:
Chief of Hampshire police says those claiming to protest over murder of Henry Nowak were ‘determined to spark fear and division’

  • UK politics live: latest news updates

Eleven officers and a police dog were injured during disorder by those claiming to protest over Henry Nowak’s murder, the chief of Hampshire police said, accusing those involved of being “determined to spark fear and division”.

A trail of destruction was left on the streets of Southampton after Tuesday night’s riot with car windows smashed and bricks strewn across roads.

Continue reading...

events.title: Trump stirs 2028 presidential election speculation with claim Vance and Rubio would be ‘unbeatable’ – US politics live
events.summary:
President tells podcast that vice-president and secretary of state ‘would be very hard to beat’ if they ran together in 2028

  • The Democrats to watch after Tuesday’s primaries

  • Sign up for the Breaking News US email

Iowa voters cast their ballots in yesterday’s heated primaries, setting up for months of fervent campaigning ahead of the November midterms in contests that could determine the balance of power in Congress.

A red state that the GOP has dominated for the past decade, Democrats believe they can be competitive in three of its four House races, its Senate election, and the contest to replace Kim Reynolds, the retiring Republican governor.

Continue reading...

events.title: Middle East crisis live: Trump claims Iranian supreme leader is involved in US negotiations
events.summary:
President says he would ‘like to meet’ Mojtaba Khamenei and appears to confirm reports he had heated call with Benjamin Netanyahu

The Kuwaiti defence ministry said it intercepted 13 ballistic missiles and 17 drones launched by Iran today.

A drone and missile attack on Kuwait’s international airport killed one person, which Kuwaiti authorities identified as an Indian national. It is the first reported death in a Gulf state since the US and Iran agreed to a ceasefire in April.

Continue reading...

events.title: California primary election results: governor’s race too close to call
events.summary:
Early results show Steve Hilton, Xavier Becerra and Tom Steyer emerging in front, with many ballots to be counted

  • US politics live: latest news updates

The high-stakes gubernatorial race in California remained too close to call on Wednesday morning, with early results showing a tight contest in the crowded race.

With many ballots still left to be counted, three candidates emerged at the top: the Republican Steve Hilton and the Democrats Xavier Becerra and Tom Steyer. Hilton was leading the field, with 28%, trailed closely by Becerra.

Continue reading...

events.title: Peabo Bryson, R&B singer behind classic Disney duets, dies aged 75
events.summary:
Two-time Grammy winner was best known for songs from Beauty and the Beast and Aladdin

Peabo Bryson, the R&B singer best known as the voice behind the Oscar-winning Disney film duets Beauty and the Beast with Celine Dion, and A Whole New World with Regina Belle from Aladdin, has died. He was 75.

His family said in a statement that Bryson, who won two Grammy awards, died on Tuesday, days after having a stroke.

Continue reading...

events.title: Karen Bass heads to LA mayoral runoff after falling short of majority
events.summary:
Incumbent comes out on top in primary election – but with less than 50% of votes – and will take on challenger in November

Karen Bass has come out ahead in Tuesday’s heated primary for Los Angeles mayor, but with less than 50% of the vote she will have to defend her seat in November’s general election.

Bass will face either Spencer Pratt, a former reality TV star, or city council member Nithya Raman, in November. As of Wednesday morning, with more than 60% of votes counted, Pratt had secured 30% of the vote, Raman had won 22%.

Continue reading...

events.title: Greetings from Porto, whose lanes are lined with colorful textiles
events.summary: Linen scarves, cotton aprons and dishtowels adorn the entrances to souvenir shops, many of which are run by Bangladeshis whose home country shares Portugal's rich tradition of textile manufacturing.

events.title: Ukrainian drones strike a St. Petersburg oil terminal ahead of Putin visit
events.summary: Ukrainian drones struck an oil terminal in St. Petersburg and set it ablaze, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said, as the city hosted an annual economic forum promoted by President Putin.

events.title: Iran and the US trade strikes in the Persian Gulf, further testing the ceasefire
events.summary: Kuwait briefly shut the country's main airport after Iranian drones heavily damaged it and killed one person, the latest in a series of attacks by Iran and the U.S. that have tested a fragile truce.

events.title: Scenes from the aftermath of Russia's latest massive attack on Kyiv
events.summary: Russian missile and drone attacks killed  civilians across Ukraine overnight, in one of Moscow's largest assaults in recent months.

events.title: EU strikes migration deal for more deportations and detention centers abroad
events.summary: The European Union has moved forward with an overhaul of its migration policy, aiming to ramp up deportations and build detention centers abroad. Critics compared the regulation to the immigration strategy of the Trump administration.

events.title: Global conflicts push Sri Lanka's economy to the brink
events.summary: Sri Lanka was careening from crisis to crisis, then came the Mideast war. People say they've got no buffer left.

events.title: Ultra-Orthodox protesters block roads and trains across Israel over military draft
events.summary: Tens of thousands of ultra-Orthodox demonstrated across Israel on Monday, blocking roads and trains and setting cars on fire to protest mandatory enlistment in Israel's military.

events.title: What to know about U.S. military strikes on alleged drug boats
events.summary: The U.S. military strikes on alleged drug boats have killed over 200 people. The attacks began in September and aim to stem drug flow, but critics question their legality and effectiveness.

events.title: Russian attack on Ukraine kills 22 people, officials say, as Moscow escalates fighting
events.summary: Russian forces launched a massive aerial attack on Kyiv and other Ukrainian cities, and officials say at least 22 civilians were killed and 138 were wounded. The assault began overnight and continued during the day.

events.title: Iran halts talks with U.S. over Israeli actions in Lebanon, Gaza
events.summary: Trumps says Israel and Hezbollah will stop fighting after Iran stops talks with U.S. over Israeli operations in southern Lebanon, Gaza

events.title: Taliban and Russia cozy up to each other — why?
events.summary: Russia and the Afghan Taliban have signed a deal on repairing Soviet-era and Russian-made weapons, a seemingly small move in a larger game involving the US, Pakistan, and Moscow's efforts to curb Western influence.

events.title: Wim Wenders withdraws film with disputed child nude scene
events.summary: Actress Nastassja Kinski spent years calling for the removal of a sexualized scene from his film "The Wrong Move," in which she appeared topless at the age of 13.

events.title: Mapping the AI narrative in Kenya and South Africa's media
events.summary: From data colonialism to deepfakes, AI is reshaping Africa. A new study shows where Kenyan and South African coverage falls short, and offers practical steps to deepen and improve reporting.

events.title: Google mosquito army: Scientists say 'we must take action'
events.summary: Google's Debug research program plans to release millions of sterile mosquitoes to fight species that spread diseases like dengue. How does the method work — and should humans interfere with nature like this?

events.title: Backrooms: The creepy internet phenomenon, explained
events.summary: A low-budget horror film directed by a 20-year-old YouTuber is beating the new Star Wars movie at the box office. Behind its success is an entire viral online maze.

events.title: Saudi Arabia and Russia draw closer amid OPEC upheaval
events.summary: As Saudi Arabia and Russia deepen cooperation, their combined influence over global oil supply is growing. Could this shift the balance of power in energy markets?

events.title: Kenya's missing children crisis exposes system gaps
events.summary: Families across Kenya are grappling with missing children cases, as gaps in child protection systems come under scrutiny and authorities dispute claims of a worsening crisis

events.title: Malaysia's social media ban for minors sparks privacy debate
events.summary: Malaysia’s ban on social media for people younger than16 has been framed as a child-safety measure, but critics warn that it could prove difficult to enforce and may come at the cost of online privacy.

events.title: Ukraine drones hit Saint Petersburg as economic forum starts
events.summary: Ukraine has been stepping up long-range strikes as the war grinds on, with Russia's westerly second city now hit. The latest attack on Saint Petersburg takes place as an event long known as "Russia's Davos" kicks off.

events.title: Germany news: Naturalizations reach record high in 2025
events.summary: Naturalizations have increased for the fifth year in a row. Automotive industry firms turn a bit less pessimistic. And Germany is aiming for a seat on the UN Security Council. Follow DW for more news from Germany.

events.title: Portugal: General strike causes disruption to services
events.summary: Flights have been canceled and schools closed as unions stage a strike over government labor reform plans. It is the second major walkout in Portugal in six months.

events.title: Who owns the billions in Germany's dormant bank accounts?
events.summary: Billions of euros sit untouched in dormant bank accounts in Germany. With heirs unaware and no central register in place, banks and politicians are debating who should ultimately control the money.

events.title: Armenia's election offers a choice between Russia and EU
events.summary: Armenians are set to vote on the country's future geopolitical direction on June 7. The election will be a test of public opinion in the long-standing Russia ally, where the population is increasingly eyeing the EU.

events.title: India news: Several killed in New Delhi fire
events.summary: At least 21 people were killed and several others injured after a fire tore through a building in New Delhi. Some of the victims were foreign nationals who had traveled to India for medical treatment. DW has more.

events.title: UK: Protest erupts over student murder
events.summary: Protesters have clashed with police in Southampton over the December murder of finance student Henry Nowak. The fatally injured victim was handcuffed by police after his killer accused him of racist abuse.

events.title: US plans extra tariffs for 60 countries over forced labor
events.summary: A new wave of Trump tariffs could unsettle US trade partners which have already signed new trade deals or have been negotiating them for months.

events.title: Democracy in Africa: Coups, crises and foregone conclusions
events.summary: As Ethiopia formally counts the votes following Monday's election, attitudes toward democracy appear to be shifting across Africa. In April, Burkina Faso's junta leader told the Burkinabe people to "forget" democracy.

events.title: World Cup: David Beckham to get Hollywood Walk of Fame star
events.summary: Former Manchester United and England superstar to be honored for role in "elevating soccer's profile in America" ahead of FIFA World Cup.

events.title: Middle East: US and Iran trade fire amid stalled talks
events.summary: The US and Iran traded renewed fire in the Persian Gulf in a flareup of tensions on Wednesday. The fighting comes as peace efforts remain stalled.

events.title: Spain's Princess Leonor makes history with parachute training, reports say
events.summary: Princess Leonor of Spain becomes the first Spanish royal to complete a parachuting course as part of her military training, according to Spanish media.

events.title: Germany: Cologne Cathedral to charge tourists €12 for entry
events.summary: Cologne Cathedral has laid out plans to charge tourist visitors, starting in July. They will be asked to pay €12 each, to help cover maintenance costs. It still plans to keep a small free area available for worshippers.

events.title: Kosovo general election: the cost of political polarization
events.summary: Sunday's election in Kosovo — the third in under 16 months — is marked by a clash between former allies PM Albin Kurti and ex-President Vjosa Osmani, deadlock in the dialogue with Serbia and obstacles to EU integration.

events.title: Unpacking Germany's campaign for a UN Security Council seat
events.summary: On Wednesday, the United Nations will hold elections for several seats on the UN Security Council. Germany's government is campaigning vigorously for a seat.

events.title: Legendary warrior women: The history of the Amazons
events.summary: They're said to have cut off their breasts to better draw their bows and even to have killed their male offspring. What is the history behind the myths surrounding the Amazons?

events.title: Ethiopia goes electric following gas car ban
events.summary: Electric vehicles have taken off in Ethiopia. Key to the shift: a world-first ban on importing fossil fuel-powered vehicles.

events.title: Refugees and migration: Is Europe closing its doors?
events.summary: Migration researchers have called on European governments to abandon isolationist policies. But many politicians are not heeding their calls.

events.title: UK wins court case over canceled plan to deport asylum seekers to Rwanda
events.summary: An international court has rejected a Rwandan claim for millions in damages over a scrapped deal to send asylum seekers to the East African nation.

events.title: Is 'nation-hopping' ruining German youth football?
events.summary: Many young German footballers are choosing other countries over Germany. DFB Director Andreas Rettig is aiming to change this and is calling for a compensation fee.

events.title: Mozart the idol: Searching for beauty in times of crisis
events.summary: Mozart's music evokes lightness and beauty. In times of war and destruction, people often invoke the ideals of the classical era. But is this just an escape from reality? The Mozartfest Würzburg offers different answers.

events.title: How China keeps North Korea's economy alive
events.summary: Pyongyang relies on China for 95% of its legitimate trade; hacking, arms deals and remittances also help it stay afloat.

events.title: Young German talents impress in World Cup tune-up vs Finland
events.summary: Deniz Undav and Lennart Karl delivered strong performances in Germany's last game at home before the start of 2026 FIFA World Cup.

events.title: German town prints its own cash to cut CO2 emissions
events.summary: It started as a school project to promote local businesses in a remote Bavarian region. Now, the "Chiemgauer" currency helps to cut emissions.

events.title: DNA had one rule. Bacteria didn't get the memo
events.summary: Every cell in every organism on Earth copies DNA the same way. Except one bacterial protein — quietly doing something scientists had never seen before.

events.title: Germany news: Most expect AfD state premier after elections
events.summary: Most people in Germany expect a premier from the far-right AfD to lead at least one state after elections in the autumn, a survey has found. The Brenner Pass linking Austria and Italy has reopened.

events.title: How the Alevi community came to thrive in Germany
events.summary: The Alevis make up the fourth largest religious community in Germany today. What are the main tenets of their faith? And how are they preserving their unique culture?

events.title: Space race: Why Portugal is reaching for the stars
events.summary: A spaceport is being built on a small Portuguese island in the Atlantic. Is Portugal on the verge of becoming a space nation?

events.title: Swing Youth: In Nazi Germany, jazz was an act of defiance
events.summary: The Nazis denounced jazz music as "degenerate art," despite its widespread popularity in Germany. As the Nazis clamped down on expression, groups of jazz-loving teenagers formed the Swing Youth to rebel.

events.title: Goodbye, Norma Jeane: How Marilyn Monroe changed Hollywood
events.summary: Long seen primarily as a sex symbol, Marilyn Monroe is now viewed as an early advocate for women in Hollywood. On the 100th anniversary of her birth, DW looks at the star who balanced fame and feminist agency.

events.title: Ukraine: IAEA seeks access to Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant after reported drone strike
events.summary: Russia claims that a Ukrainian drone struck the Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant. Ukraine has denied the accusation. The International Atomic Energy Agency has sought to examine the facility.

events.title: PSG's team of talents retain Champions League title
events.summary: Paris Saint-Germain were unable to score from open play against a resilient Arsenal but won their second successive title on penalties. Luis Enrique has built a team to be proud of at the oil-rich club.

events.title: Film funding bill to support blockbusters made in Germany
events.summary: The German government wants to attract more filmmakers and make the country a "global player" in on-screen entertainment. A new bill would require media companies to make mandatory investments in the film industry.

events.title: Germany: Vaping becoming more popular among young people
events.summary: A recent study shows that e-cigarettes are becoming increasingly popular among young people in Germany, with even elementary school kids picking up vapes. Health experts are alarmed.

events.title: What Hungary must do to receive EU funds frozen under Orban
events.summary: Prime Minister Peter Magyar and European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen announced a deal that could free up over €16 billion in EU funds for Hungary. Now, Magyar's new government needs to deliver.

events.title: Russia recalls Armenia ambassador over EU ties
events.summary: The recall comes a day after Putin warned Armenia against seeking EU membership and alluded to the "Ukrainian scenario." Armenia historically has strong ties with Russia but has pivoted toward the West in recent years.

events.title: Malta votes in snap parliamentary election
events.summary: Voting is underway in Malta's parliamentary election. Opinion polls show Prime Minister Robert Abela on course for reelection in the shadow of the Middle East crisis.

events.title: International aid cuts complicate Congo's Ebola epidemic
events.summary: The scaling back of development aid has contributed to the inability to contain the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo. German organizations are calling for a major spending boost to help stop the spread.

events.title: Romania's political crisis deepens
events.summary: After Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan’s government fell in early May, Romania's moderate parties have struggled to chart a new course — opening the door for the far right to gain ground.

events.title: EU unblocks €16 billion in Hungary assistance as Magyar promises reforms
events.summary: Reforms under new Prime Minister Peter Magyar will enable the EU to free up billions in frozen assistance for Budapest. The EU's Ursula von der Leyen announced the news as Magyar visited Brussels.

events.title: What makes a vanity project? A history of building for power
events.summary: As Donald Trump proposes costly redesigns for Washington, DC, the question arises: When do grand projects serve the public — and when do they serve the leader?

events.title: Kai Havertz: Arsenal and Germany's man for the big moment
events.summary: Germany forward Kai Havertz is set to play in his second Champions League final. The Arsenal man is a match winner.

events.title: Germany deepens Taiwan ties amid China tensions
events.summary: A German delegation, known as the Berlin-Taipei Parliamentary Friendship Group, recently visited Taiwan, drawing criticism from Beijing. Two lawmakers told DW the visit was important for expanding economic partnerships.

events.title: Pigeons' livers may enable magnetic navigation when the sun doesn't shine, study finds
events.summary: A research team in Germany has published new data on how pigeons use magnetic fields as a navigation method when visibility is limited. A key part of the long-sought answer could lie in an unexpected organ, their livers.

events.title: The Superbug spill: When medicine goes rogue
events.summary: Pharmaceuticals have been found in waterways almost everywhere in the world, fueling antibiotic-resistant superbugs that no longer respond to treatment. Is there a way out?

events.title: France moves to symbolically repeal slavery legislation
events.summary: Members of the lower house of the French parliament unanimously voted to abolish a series of royal edicts known as the "Code noir" or Black Code. The bill stops short of some lawmakers' demands like possible reparations.

events.title: Suez, Panama canals charge for transit — why can't Hormuz?
events.summary: Iran has been widely condemned for demanding up to $2 million for vessels to ship through the Strait of Hormuz. DW explores the reasons why Egypt can charge for the Suez Canal and Panama for its waterway, but Iran can't.

events.title: Reiche brings energy sector ties to German Economy Ministry
events.summary: Economy Minister Reiche is a controversial figure. Her plans for new gas-fired power plants, rolling back plans for more renewables in the building sector and scrapping solar subsidies have been met with criticism.

events.title: UK re-chalks Dorset's famed and feisty Cerne Abbas Giant
events.summary: Britain's National Trust is working on replacing the chalk outline of the Cerne Abbas Giant in Dorset. The Saxon-era carving depicts a club-wielding man "still standing tall," as the charity puts it.

events.title: Why Europe is getting so hot
events.summary: Europe is sweltering under extreme heat that has broken records and claimed lives. Why is the continent heating so fast?

events.title: New Ebola cases in Congo: What you need to know
events.summary: The virus spreads from direct person-to-person contact. But here's what makes it especially lethal: it persists in corpses, and funeral practices often take place precisely when bodies are most infectious.

events.title: Iran war: Oil shortages threaten global energy security
events.summary: Strategic petroleum reserves are declining rapidly as countries release emergency crude supplies to cushion the economic impact of the Middle East conflict.

events.title: FIFA World Cup: New York and New Jersey prosecutors probe 'sky-high' ticket prices
events.summary: US prosecutors have subpoenaed FIFA over expensive World Cup tickets, saying the event is "not an invitation to exploit our residents and visitors."

events.title: Germany: No recovery in sight for the economy
events.summary: The war in Iran has dashed hopes for economic growth. Germany's pension and healthcare systems are also feeling the strain.

events.title: EU enlargement: Is the bloc ready to welcome new members?
events.summary: Brussels says the EU must grow, but resistance within the bloc remains strong, with some arguing that it must reform before enlarging. Montenegro remains the only country with a realistic chance of joining soon.

events.title: Turkey: Leadership dispute engulfs opposition CHP
events.summary: The top figures in Turkey's main opposition party differ on how to resolve the leadership vacuum after a court annulled its 2023 primary. One wants the party delegates to pick a leader, the other wants members to vote.

events.title: Europe's energy problem isn't green power — it's storage
events.summary: Solar and wind produce lots of energy — but not always at the right time. More battery storage could help Europe to stabilize prices and replace polluting fossil fuel energy, but roadblocks remain.

events.title: Germany eyes heat pumps as Iran war drives energy costs up
events.summary: The war in the Middle East is threatening fossil fuel supplies and causing energy prices to rise in Germany. Could that drive a surge in demand for heat pumps?

events.title: Spain: Police raid HQ of ruling PSOE Socialist party in graft probe
events.summary: Police have searched the headquarters of Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez's PSOE party investigating possible illegal payments. Sanchez meanwhile said he was still standing by former PM Jose Luis Rodriguez Zapatero.

events.title: License to play: The new James Bond video game
events.summary: James Bond is back, and he's as charismatic and daring as ever. The video game "007 First Light" tells the origin story of the British secret agent — and the stakes are high.

events.title: Germany: Alleged leftist militant found guilty of robbery
events.summary: Daniela Klette, suspected to be one of the last remaining members of the left-wing terrorist group Red Army Faction, has been found guilty for her part in a series of robberies — but more serious charges await.

events.title: The German gym fighting the far right with MMA
events.summary: The far right is increasingly using MMA to recruit new members. But a gym in Chemnitz, in the east of Germany, is fighting back with a martial arts program focused on diversity and empowerment.

events.title: How did Berlin's Jewish Hospital and hundreds of its patients survive the Holocaust?
events.summary: When Red Army troops entered Berlin's Jewish Hospital in 1945, they were astonished to discover hundreds of Jews living just a couple of miles away from Hitler's bunker.

events.title: Ukraine: EU, Germany summon Russian envoys after Moscow tells diplomats to leave Kyiv
events.summary: The EU and Germany both summoned Russian diplomats to protest attacks on Kyiv and orders for diplomats to leave the city. The UN secretary-general also rebuked Russia in a session on proliferating conflicts worldwide.

events.title: Ferrari unveils its first all-electric car, the 4-door Luce
events.summary: The Italian supercar giant has released images of what will be both its first ever pure EV, and its first car that can seat five people. The Luce eyes a younger clientele and the increasingly competitive Chinese market.

events.title: Putin offers debt relief to new recruits for Ukraine war
events.summary: New recruits for Russia's war in Ukraine will have debts up to 10 million rubles canceled under a presidential decree. Another new law reportedly foresees the use of Russian armed forces to protect citizens abroad.

events.title: Belgium: Train collides with school bus, several dead
events.summary: A train has crashed into a school bus near a town close to Brussels, killing at least four people, including two children.

events.title: The wind boom Trump couldn't stop
events.summary: Even with fierce opposition, offshore wind projects are multiplying along US coasts — signaling a massive shift in energy.

events.title: Berlin Hauptbahnhof at 20: A rail hub with symbolic power
events.summary: Berlin's central station was inaugurated in May 2006. Beyond its functionality, the modern glass-and-steel structure was built to play a key role in reunified Germany.

events.title: Will SpaceX's IPO fund life on Mars — and a trillionaire?
events.summary: SpaceX is launching history's biggest public listing, propelling Elon Musk's wealth into the stratosphere. DW explores whether the firm's out-of-this-world ambitions are truly cosmic or another black hole for investors.

events.title: Germany news: More than 1,900 missing children
events.summary: Authorities say there are 1,933 missing children cases nationwide, up 6.8% from the year before. And, travellers in Germany seem nervous when it comes to air travel, amid the war in Iran, a survey suggests.

events.title: Germany's Dr. Wolff cosmetics company is going all in on AI
events.summary: This medium-sized, family-run business has embraced artificial intelligence to stay competitive. All employees are encouraged to learn about and apply LLMs.

events.title: Blinded by the light pollution: Cities seek to restore night
events.summary: Artificial lights are blotting out the moon and stars, harming human health, and disrupting the natural world. Why is this a problem? And how can we stop it without sitting in the dark?

events.title: Germany plans to address growing use of date-rape drugs
events.summary: Germany's justice minister is seeking harsher penalties for the use of date-rape drugs in sexual assaults. But victims say more needs to be done about a growing problem.

events.title: Enhanced Games: Why 'crippling injuries' are the least of it
events.summary: Doping in sports is nothing new. Athletes at the Enhanced Games will know the risks — severe injury, even paralysis. But bioethicists are also concerned we're opening the gates to altering our fundamental human biology.

events.title: What is Pentecost?
events.summary: People new to Germany are often surprised by the number of public holidays in the spring. Pentecost is one of them, but its significance is not well-known.

events.title: Germany: Record numbers take German passport in 2025
events.summary: A record number of people received a German passport in 2025, a media report says. Numbers have been rising since Germany changed citizenship requirements in 2024.

events.title: Germany's 'rapid militarization' threatens basic rights
events.summary: Ten human rights organizations say security interests are increasingly being prioritized over fundamental rights in Germany. The scathing criticism was presented in their "2026 Fundamental Rights Report."

events.title: Kevin Warsh: Trump ally at the Federal Reserve?
events.summary: Never before has a chair of the US Federal Reserve been so controversial. Who is Kevin Warsh? Can he preserve the Fed's independence, or will he become Donald Trump's puppet?

events.title: US or Iran: Who will win the Hormuz endurance game?
events.summary: Tehran is betting that its sanctions‑hardened economy can outlast the Strait of Hormuz crisis. Washington, meanwhile, is contending with elevated oil prices, renewed inflation pressure and rising global recession risks.

events.title: The dying dream of the dead to be at peace with life
events.summary: Many people have vivid dreams shortly before death. Research suggests the dreams are not a sign of confusion — but may help both the dying and their loved ones make sense of loss.

events.title: How the Cannes 2026 films reflect a world in conflict
events.summary: Various works explore the impact of authoritarianism through a historical lens, while one thriller on corruption is set in Putin's Russia.

events.title: DW exclusive: Germany's far-right AfD vows to 'make history'
events.summary: The AfD looks set to take power in a German state for the first time. In a DW exclusive, the front-runner in Saxony-Anhalt discusses plans to detain immigrants and revamp the school system, and relations with Russia.

events.title: Ebola patient in Berlin: Why Germany's helping
events.summary: An Ebola‑infected US doctor is being treated under the highest safety standards in a specialized isolation unit in the German capital, Berlin. The facilities are considered to be among the best equipped worldwide.

events.title: Will Germany's aviation tax cut lower ticket prices?
events.summary: The tax cut aims to ease the burden on the aviation industry and make flying slightly cheaper. However, some experts warn that it is unlikely to have a meaningful impact on ticket prices.

events.title: Germany 2026 World Cup squad announcement: Neuer returns
events.summary: Two years after retiring from international football, Manuel Neuer is back in Germany's 2026 World Cup squad.

events.title: Extreme climate scenario fades, but warming continues
events.summary: The rapid rollout of renewable energy is helping to shift emissions trends, but expected temperature rises remain high as the UN moves to tighten countries' commitments.

events.title: Freiburg's Europa League final proof another way can work
events.summary: Bundesliga side Freiburg might have lost their first-ever European final to Aston Villa, but their European run is a reminder that success in football can be achieved without big spending or a loss of identity.

events.title: Why Stephen Colbert's 'Late Show' mattered
events.summary: As the final curtain falls on "The Late Show," a brief history of the evolution of the late-night tradition and Stephen Colbert's satire.

events.title: Researchers spot humpback whales off Australia and Brazil — longest lifetime journey on record
events.summary: Researchers have observed two humpback whales swimming between Australia and Brazil. It's the longest travel distance humans have ever recorded for the species.

events.title: How Meta wants to profile 13-year-olds on Insta, Facebook
events.summary: Instagram and Facebook have a problem with underage users — there's too many of them. Parent company, Meta, aims to root out under-13s with an age-profiling AI. This plays into a debate about a teenage social media ban.

events.title: EU is failing to cut pesticide use
events.summary: With binding cut targets scrapped, the European Union's pledge to halve pesticide use is stalling — even as controversial chemicals like glyphosate remain on sale across the bloc.

events.title: Enhanced Games: Pushing human potential, or lethal circus?
events.summary: Investors like Peter Thiel and Donald Trump Jr. see the Enhanced Games as proof of how far human limits can stretch. For doping experts, the danger is clear and grave. DW explains the latest controversial sporting event.

events.title: Pioneer of pop music: Cher turns 80
events.summary: Cher has had countless hits and sparked more than a few controversies — while always doing things her own way. At 80, she remains as iconic as ever.

events.title: Hearing aids may cut noise by reading brain signals
events.summary: In a crowded room, it can be impossible for hearing aid users to focus on what they want or need to hear. Research into auditory neurotechnology may enhance sounds and voices and reduce noise in real-time.

events.title: How China became Russia's economic lifeline
events.summary: Hot on the heels of Donald Trump's visit, Russian President Vladimir Putin travels to Beijing for talks with Xi Jinping. DW explores how China's trade and financial support is helping keep Russia's economy afloat.

events.title: Germany's crisis-hit chemical industry seeks revival
events.summary: Hit by high energy costs and regulation, Germany's chemical companies are scaling back production at home while expanding abroad. Can the trend be reversed?

events.title: Elversberg: Bundesliga newcomers from tiny town make history
events.summary: A team from a small southwestern German town of just 7,000 inhabitants has made history by sealing promotion to become the 59th club to play in the Bundesliga.

events.title: Ebola in Africa: Why it's a constant threat
events.summary: Ebola is highly contagious and deadly. It spreads internationally, such as in the May 2026 outbreaks in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda. But that doesn't automatically make it a pandemic.

events.title: Germany and Japan test hydrogen future with BMW, Toyota cars
events.summary: During a recent visit to Japan, Germany's transport minister Patrick Schnieder toured hydrogen projects aimed at bringing the fuel into the mainstream.

events.title: 'From collection to connection': How museums are redefining themselves
events.summary: Gone are the days when museums were just places to see art and artifacts. On International Museum Day, DW looks at how museums are becoming unique spaces of community engagement and participation.

events.title: Andreas Rettig: 'You can't separate sports and politics'
events.summary: The World Cup in the USA, Canada and Mexico is sparking discussions beyond the realm of football. DFB Director Andreas Rettig discusses the Germany team and the geopolitical situation in an interview with DW.

events.title: Iconic German musician Udo Lindenberg turns 80
events.summary: From a historic East German concert to a hit with rapper Apache 207, Udo Lindenberg has shaped German rock music like few others. Even at 80, the "Panikrocker" is still reinventing himself.

events.title: Asteroid 2026 JH2 — no need to worry about it hitting Earth
events.summary: This big asteroid was discovered shortly before it was expected to hurtle past Earth. Any time you see a headline about asteroids approaching the planet, you can relax. Big and small asteroids fly by all the time.

events.title: 'FIFA got greedy' ― Why football fans in India may not be able to watch World Cup games on TV this year
events.summary: The 2026 World Cup is weeks away, but one of the most populated countries in the world doesn't have a broadcasting deal. Why?

events.title: Lab-grown Tyrannosaurus leather: More chicken than dinosaur?
events.summary: A handbag is promoted as the "world's first T. rex leather product". But scientists are questioning its authenticity.

events.title: Who is Mario Draghi, this year's Charlemagne Prize winner?
events.summary: The Charlemagne Prize honors those who advance European unity. This year's winner is Italy's Mario Draghi, a former European Central Bank chief and a key architect of European stability during a time of economic crisis.

events.title: Honda reports first loss since 1957 as it waters down EV strategy, but shares rise on 2026 forecasts
events.summary: The Japanese carmaker pursued an aggressive switch towards electric vehicles that it's now trying to walk back. Despite posting its first loss since 1957, its share price spiked on more buoyant predictions for 2026.

events.title: China goes electric, but can it get off coal?
events.summary: The world's clean energy superpower also emits the most CO2. But coal-fired power may have peaked as China electrifies its economy with ever more renewables.

events.title: US drops bond requirement for FIFA World Cup ticket holders
events.summary: Fans from five African World Cup qualifying nations with valid tickets are exempt from paying a hefty bond. But fans from several other countries still face major hurdles to seeing their teams play in the United States.

events.title: Fake TikTok fans and pop music's 'unethical' marketing
events.summary: Do indie darlings like Sombr and Geese owe their success to TikTok "trend simulations"? The question has sent shockwaves through their fan base. Yet manipulated charts are as old as the music business itself.

events.title: China’s new rules give the West a new headache
events.summary: Beijing now has strong new powers to punish firms for pulling manufacturing out of China. Multinationals are increasingly caught in a web of coercion and complex rules as they try to operate across the US, EU and China.

events.title: Satellites add a new layer to global poverty data
events.summary: A new study using satellite data finds that 58% of the global population has been assigned to the wrong tier in the UN's Human Development Index. That can have real-world consequences for how aid reaches people.

events.title: Why men are less worried than women about climate change
events.summary: From petro-masculinity to electric trucks: why men disengage from climate action and what might bring them back.

events.title: Hotter 2026 and El Nino could trigger extreme fires
events.summary: More than 150 million hectares — over twice the size of Texas — burned globally in the first months of 2026. With a high chance of a supercharged El Nino, the second half of the year could be even worse.

events.title: Bundesliga: Kathleen Krüger appointed Hamburg's head of sport
events.summary: Hamburg have appointed Kathleen Krüger as the club's new board member responsible for sport. She becomes the first woman to fill such a role at a Bundesliga club on a permanent basis.

events.title: Sport helping displaced Lebanese living in stadium to cope
events.summary: More than a million people have been forced to flee their homes in southern Lebanon due to fighting between Israeli forces and Hezbollah. As many shelter in Beirut stadiums, sport is sometimes a welcome distraction.

events.title: Hijab-wearing German beauty contestant takes on the AfD
events.summary: As a Miss Germany contestant, Büsra Sayed wore a hijab on stage. This had never happened before — and the far-right AfD didn't like it. Her response went viral.

events.title: Hantavirus: How is the outbreak being contained?
events.summary: Passengers evacuated from the MV Hondius cruise ship are returning to their home countries, some with symptoms, many without. What happens to these people and what is being done to contain the spread?

events.title: Architecture of remembrance and hope: Daniel Libeskind at 80
events.summary: Famous for designing the Jewish Museum Berlin and the Ground Zero project in New York, the architect has created dozens of memorable buildings worldwide.

events.title: Hantavirus: How it differs from COVID
events.summary: In 2018-2019, the Andes virus hantavirus spread person-to-person in Argentina. Eleven people died — but it was stopped. Analysis of that outbreak shows the current one can also be stopped.

events.title: Endemic, epidemic, pandemic: What's the difference?
events.summary: The Andes hantavirus outbreak shows how easily disease labels are misunderstood — and why they describe patterns of spread, not how dangerous a virus is.

events.title: Somali piracy disrupts global shipping and trade routes
events.summary: As ships reroute around Africa to avoid Middle East conflict zones, piracy off Somalia is back. The resurgence is driving up insurance, transit times and security costs, putting new pressure on global supply chains.

events.title: Why is Hollywood missing from Cannes?
events.summary: There are no US blockbusters premiering at the Cannes Film Festival this year. So who's snubbing who?

events.title: New film portrays Thomas and Erika Mann — with an oddly twisted premise
events.summary: Premiering at Cannes and starring Sandra Hüller, Pawel Pawlikowski's "Fatherland" is set during the Cold War — a complex period for the prominent anti-Nazi intellectuals.

events.title: Eurovision 2026: Politics collide with a blockbuster show
events.summary: Controversy over Israel's participation and boycotts unprecedented in scale are already weighing on the Eurovision Song Contest before it even kicks off in Vienna.

events.title: Celebrated broadcaster and naturalist David Attenborough turns 100
events.summary: The still-sprightly centenarian has been knighted twice, nominated for the 2022 Nobel Peace Prize, and is perhaps the world's most recognizable nature lover.

events.title: World Cup 'prices will drop' but too late for traveling fans
events.summary: Among many firsts at World Cup 2026, FIFA has tried its hand at dynamic pricing and acting as a secondary seller. The tactic has seen a ticket listed for over $2 million, but many games seem undersold. What's going on?

events.title: Why the EU sees Chinese solar tech as a major security risk
events.summary: Europe's solar boom is powered by Chinese technology. But experts warn Chinese-made tech could threaten the continent's safety and even create blackouts. Now, Brussels is aiming to reduce its dependence.

events.title: Bayern Munich exit Champions League in semifinal to PSG
events.summary: Holders Paris Saint-Germain are one game away from retaining the Champions League after knocking out German champions Bayern Munich. Bayern can still complete the domestic double but again miss out on the big one.

events.title: COVID pioneer BioNTech: The fall of Germany's pandemic hero
events.summary: BioNTech, once celebrated for its COVID-19 vaccines, is grappling with falling demand, restructuring and the departure of its founders. Can its ambitious cancer trials turn it from a one-hit wonder into a biotech giant?

events.title: Hungary's new government pushes for euro by 2030
events.summary: Hungary's new leadership wants the country to adopt the euro by the end of the decade while repairing strained ties with Brussels. But with a weak economy and tight deadlines, experts warn the path will be steep.

events.title: What is hantavirus?
events.summary: Three people have died on a cruise ship in the Atlantic, reportedly from hantavirus. The dangerous Andes strain has been confirmed in two cases. What are the symptoms, and how is it transmitted?

events.title: The sea that is vanishing in real time
events.summary: The world's largest inland body of water is retreating at an alarming speed. From stranded buildings to vanishing habitats, scientists warn the Caspian Sea may be approaching a tipping point.

events.title: Russia: Ukraine drones hit Saint Petersburg
events.summary: Ukrainian drones hit energy and military sites in Saint Petersburg early Wednesday morning as officials gathered for a flagship economic forum in the city, Russian and Ukrainian authorities said.

events.title: Shnaider beats Sabalenka at French Open to make semi-finals
Shnaider beats Sabalenka at French Open to make semi-finals
events.summary: World No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka was eliminated on Wednesday in the quarterfinals of the French Open, losing to Russia's Diana Shnaider (3-6, 7-5, 6-0).

events.title: 'Palestinians are unbelievable people: Vast majority of in Gaza just want to live a normal life'
events.summary: Angela Diffly is pleased to welcome Dr. Adi Nadimpalli, Medical Coordinator for Gaza with Doctors Without Borders. He offers a stark assessment of conditions in Gaza following the October 2025 ceasefire. While acknowledging that "the number of kinetic injuries and violence has decreased," he argues that the territory remains trapped in a state of profound humanitarian deterioration, marked by daily strikes, mass displacement, inadequate food supplies, collapsing living conditions, and widespread insecurity.

events.title: Israel and Hezbollah exchange fire amid new talks in Washington
events.summary: Lebanon said Israeli strikes on June 3 killed at least nine people in the country's south, while the Israeli army says it has intercepted a "hostile aircraft" in its northern regions. This comes as a new round of talks is to be held in Washington. FRANCE 24's Renée Davis in Beirut and Noga Tarnopolsky in Jerusalem tell us more.

events.title: Trump denies impasse in Iran talks after fresh strikes on Gulf countries
events.summary: The ceasefire with Iran is being further tested after Iranian drones hit a terminal building in Kuwait. It forced the country to close down the airport briefly and one person was killed and at least 63 injured, according to the Kuwaiti health ministry. Meanwhile, talks with the US have been stalling over Lebanon and the US military said it had launched strikes on an island in the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Ukrainian drones hit Saint Petersburg as 'Russian Davos' opens in the city
events.summary: Ukrainian long-range drones hit an oil terminal in the Russian city of St Petersburg on June 3, setting the facility ablaze. It came hours before Vladimir Putin's showcase economic forum got under way in the city, apparently an attempt to embarrass the Kremlin chief. Moscow said it would keep striking Ukraine systematically in response to such attacks.

events.title: Film show: 'DJ Ahmet' charms with sheep, grief and a rave
events.summary: Critic Emma Jones tells us why North Macedonian production "DJ Ahmet", starring first-time actors, is a gem of an independent movie and proof that the small country punches well above its weight when it comes to cinema. We also discuss unsettling thriller "The Plague", starring Joel Edgerton, as a group of teenage boys at a swimming camp prompts comparisons with "Lord of The Flies"-style dynamics. Plus, body horror "Saccharine" explores contemporary obsessions with weight loss and body image, and the "Scary Movie" horror spoof franchise returns for a sixth outing, 26 years after its Y2K debut.

events.title: Under Paris's Notre Dame, archaeologists are unearthing 2,000 years of history
events.summary: Since Notre Dame Cathedral reopened to the public in 2024 following a devastating fire, it has been one of the most popular tourist destinations in Paris. What many don’t realize is that the work continues near the cathedral underground. To make way for trees and shade on the square in front of the church, workers have undertaken a significant archaeological dig.

events.title: US finds huge tunnel on the Mexican border used to smuggle drugs
events.summary: Mexican authorities have discovered an illegal tunnel that is 600 metres long on the US border with Mexico. It links Tijuana with the city of San Diego. Its purpose? Possibly the illegal smuggling of drugs. The discovery was the result of a six-month long investigation.

events.title: Wembanyama chases NBA dream ahead of Spurs-Nicks final
events.summary: The NBA finals start on June 4 and the San Antonio Spurs, with its star player, Victor Wembanyama, are favoured to take the title. But as the 22-year-old Frenchman told reporters, it's not a done deal yet against the Knicks - who haven't won since 1973 but won 11 consecutive games in the playoffs this year.

events.title: Ukraine strikes St. Petersburg with drones ahead of 'Russian Davos'
events.summary: Ukrainian drone attacks in St. Petersburg targeted military and energy infrastructure overnight into Wednesday, setting ablaze a Russian warship and oil depots on the Baltic coast. This attack deep in Russian territory came on the opening day of Putin's three-day economic forum held in the city and as NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte  arrived in Kyiv for an unannounced visit.

events.title: Aid worker Elsa Softic on Lebanon's worsening humanitarian crisis
events.summary: As millions of Lebanese remain displaced from their homes amid the ongoing war between Israel and Hezbollah, Elsa Softic, deputy chief of operations at Première Urgence Internationale, told FRANCE 24 how her organisation is helping those affected. She spoke to us in Perspective.

events.title: UK: Outrage grows after police handcuff dying student
events.summary: British police faced national backlash over the inflammatory case of an 18-year-old student who was handcuffed as he lay dying from stab wounds after his killer falsely alleged a racist attack. The killing of Henry Nowak happened in December, but the story got renewed attention after the killer was sentenced to life in prison on Monday, and video was released that showed police not believing Nowak when he said that he had been stabbed.

events.title: Benin's creators revive national history through video games
events.summary: In Benin, creators are turning to digital games to help young people learn about their country's history and explore their cultural heritage in a new way - whether through video games or augmented reality.

events.title: Kenyans protest femicide scourge and denounce government inaction
events.summary: Hundreds of protesters marched through Kenya's capital on June 1 against the high number of killings of women and children. At least 69 women have been killed since the beginning of the year, with official data also recording over 10,000 missing children over the past sixteen months.

events.title: Kenyan opposition to Ebola quarantine centre for US citizens grows
events.summary: In Kenya, the High Court has extended the suspension order against the implementation of a controversial agreement between Kenya and the United States that includes plans for an Ebola quarantine facility for Americans suspected of being infected with the virus. This comes amid protests over said centre, during which two people have died. The court has also prohibited the transfer of patients from third countries to Kenya. FRANCE 24's Bastien Renouil tells us more from Nairobi.

events.title: Strikes on Russian strategic sites show Ukraine 'ramping up long-range strikes'
events.summary: Ukrainian drones hit energy and military sites in Saint Petersburg as officials gathered for a flagship economic forum in the city. The strikes come a day after a barrage of Russian missiles and drones killed 23 people across Ukraine. FRANCE 24's Kethevane Gorjestani looks at why those strikes are so significant.

events.title: Pope names first lay woman as head of a Vatican department
events.summary: For the first time in Vatican history, a lay woman will head the Vatican’s Dicastery for Communication. Maria Montserrat Alvarado, CEO of a Catholic television network, will be in charge of the vast apparatus surrounding the Pope. Television, radio, broadcasting... A clear commitment to continuing Pope Francis’ legacy of modernizing the Catholic Church and giving women a greater role.

events.title: 'El Niño turbocharging climate change', warns Gareth Redmond-King
events.summary: Oliver Farry is pleased to welcome Gareth Redmond-King, Head of the International Programme at the Energy & Climate Intelligence Unit. As the World Meteorological Organization warns of a powerful El Niño event and its potentially far-reaching consequences, Redmond-King warns that El Niño should not be viewed as an isolated threat, but rather as an accelerant acting upon an already destabilised climate system. El Niño is "pouring fuel on that particular fire" of climate change. He describes the phenomenon as "turbocharging climate change" by adding heat to a warming planet.

events.title: Israel strikes hospital in southern Lebanon
events.summary: Israel continues to hit targets in Lebanon despite a partial US-brokered ceasefire in place. Lebanese and Israeli diplomats are in Washington for the fourth round of direct talks, but those negotiations have been strongly opposed by Hezbollah. Pressure also mounting within Netanyahu's own government to continue the war in Lebanon.

events.title: Influencers poised to shake up France's 2027 presidential race
events.summary: French influencers are set to play a key role in the 2027 presidential campaign. More than half of under-25s cite social media and video platforms as their main source of information, according to a survey published in January 2026 by French media regulator Arcom.

events.title: Chinese-linked tech company uses AI to predict who might become a dissident
events.summary: PRESS REVIEW – Wednesday, June 3, 2026: The British front pages discuss the murder of 18-year-old student Henry Nowak after the release of shocking police bodycam footage. Next, the Trump administration's appointment of the new director of national intelligence is getting a lot of coverage. Also, The Times reveals that a Chinese state-linked tech company is using AI to predict who might become a dissident. Finally, football and fashion mix on the way to the World Cup.

events.title: 'I can't breathe': protesters clash with police over handcuffed dying student
events.summary: Protests erupted in the UK after a video emerged of a dying student being handcuffed by police. Eighteen-year-old Henry Nowak was heard repeatedly telling officers: "I can't breathe" after he was stabbed by a Sikh man who falsely accused him of racial abuse. The far right have seized on the case, saying white British people are treated as second class citizens by the police. This is despite a UK government report in 2024 which found that black people were 2.2 times as likely to be arrested as white people. The UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer said the bodycam footage was "harrowing" and there would be an investigation into police conduct.

events.title: A US-Iran conflict was wargamed 14 years ago - here's how it went
events.summary: 👉Listen to The World with Richard Engel and Yalda Hakim on your podcast app👈

events.title: 'Keir Starmer made us promises - and we thought they'd be kept'
events.summary: Two boys from Gaza who received medical treatment in the UK have said the prime minister has "broken a promise" to reunite them with their families and bring over hundreds of injured children.

events.title: Two dead in Kenya after protests against US planned Ebola quarantine zone
events.summary: Two people have died in Kenya during protests against a US-proposed quarantine facility for Ebola patients, according to the organiser.

events.title: Man accused of plotting 20 Iran-linked terror attacks in Europe and Canada appears in court
events.summary: An Iraqi man has been accused of plotting at least 18 terror attacks across Europe, according to charges filed by the US Department of Justice (DOJ).

events.title: British couple jailed in Iran lose appeal, family says
events.summary: A British couple jailed on spying charges in Iran have lost an appeal against their convictions, their family has said.

events.title: If Cuba collapses, Trump will be forced to deal with the consequences of his actions
events.summary: It's only a couple of months or so since I was last in Cuba, and conditions then were pretty dire: power cuts, large queues for bread and fuel, state-run grocery stores virtually empty, mounting piles of rubbish on street corners, and few foreign visitors.

events.title: UK Royal Navy says 3 members died in a helicopter crash during a training exercise
events.summary: Britain’s Royal Navy said three of its members died in a helicopter crash earlier in southwest England during a training exercise

events.title: Australian court bans man from contacting Norwegian princess studying in Sydney
events.summary: A court in Australia has banned a 63-year-old man from contacting Norway's Princess Ingrid Alexander or her family for two years as she studies at university in Sydney

events.title: A Hong Kong artist tries to mark the Tiananmen crackdown. He was quickly stopped by police
events.summary: A performance artist in Hong Kong has tried to display a red thread to remember the victims of the 1989 Tiananmen Square crackdown

events.title: Germany seizes tons of cocaine and suspects are arrested in Spain
events.summary: German authorities have seized over 8 metric tons of cocaine from a container meant to carry cacao beans

events.title: Rare albino buffalo named after Donald Trump for its golden locks draws crowds
events.summary: A rare albino buffalo fondly named after Donald Trump for its distinctive blond tuft is drawing a huge crowd at the national zoo in Bangladesh’s capital

events.title: UK government condemns violence at protest over teen's stabbing death
events.summary: Britain’s interior minister accuses activists of hijacking a tragedy to stir up violence after police were attacked at a protest over a teenager's death

events.title: Australian judges weigh Indigenous activist’s bid to prosecute King Charles for genocide
events.summary: Three Australian appeals court judges have reserved their decision on whether an activist can prosecute King Charles III for alleged genocide of Australia’s Indigenous people

events.title: Colombia's soccer jersey isn't just about sports. It's become a symbol of political division
events.summary: Colombia’s national soccer jersey has become the subject of controversy during the country's bitterly disputed presidential election

events.title: Raúl Castro turns 95 as Cuba celebrates enigmatic figure
events.summary: Raúl Castro has turned 95, a landmark birthday for a man still helping lead one of the last communist countries in the world

events.title: Fire sweeps through a New Delhi building, killing at least 21 people
events.summary: A fire has swept through a building in New Delhi, killing at least 21 people and injuring several others

events.title: Former Thai prime minister Thaksin Shinawatra freed early from parole after receiving royal pardon
events.summary: Thailand’s former Prime Minister Thaksin Shinawatra has completed his obligations related to a prison sentence

events.title: Iran and the US trade strikes in the Persian Gulf, further testing ceasefire

events.summary: Iran launched a deadly new set of attacks in the Persian Gulf on Wednesday as it traded strikes with the United States, the latest exchange to threaten the fragile ceasefire and stalled peace talks between the two countries

events.title: Graphic: Track U.S., state and county gas prices
events.summary: Are gas prices declining? NBC News is tracking national, state and county prices at the pump.

events.title: Map: See how gas prices have changed in the county where you live
events.summary: How much gas prices have increased during the Iran war depends entirely on where you live, an NBC News analysis of average gas price data from AAA shows.

events.title: Huge attack on Ukraine leaves more than 20 dead
events.summary: Hundreds of drones and dozens of missiles launched by Russia killed at least 22 people according to Ukrainian officials. NBC News’ Keir Simmons reports on the warnings more attacks could come tonight.

events.title: Bear attacks in Japan on the rise
events.summary: Video shows a young man being chased and then knocked to the ground by a bear outside a workplace in Fukushima, Japan. It’s part of a frightening rise in bear attacks across Japan. NBC News’ Camila Bernal reports on what’s behind the attacks.

events.title: Russia targets Kyiv with massive overnight attack
events.summary: More than a dozen people were killed and hundreds more inured after an overnight barrage of Russian missiles and drones targeted the Ukrainian capital. Russia had previously warned that it intended “systematic strikes” on targets in Kyiv linked to Ukraine’s military, urging foreigners to leave.

events.title: BTS fans pray to Taoist God of Love in hopes of getting concert tickets
events.summary: BTS fans in Taiwan are seen praying to the Taoist love deity, as tickets for the Taiwan show begin presale this week. It comes fresh off BTS's big win at the American Music Awards last week. NBC News' Jesse Kirsch reports.

events.title: Trump defrosts relations with Europe’s last dictator, Alexander Lukashenko
events.summary: The man known as Europe’s last dictator may soon be coming in from the cold as President Donald Trump helps him emerge from years of isolation.

events.title: From solar to charcoal, Cubans look for alternatives amid outages and fuel shortages
events.summary: Some Cubans who’ve been able to buy solar panels say it’s a game changer, but most rely on other alternatives including charcoal, despite health concerns and the steep cost.

events.title: Absence of USAID likely slowed Ebola detection and response, former officials say
events.summary: The Trump administration hollowed out USAID last year, but the State Department denied the changes have hampered its response.

events.title: Submit your questions for NBC News’ Keir Simmons
events.summary: NBC News’ chief international correspondent will be in St. Petersburg, Russia, for a live Q&A. Submit your questions here.

events.title: Tracking the Ebola outbreak: Where the deadly virus is spreading
events.summary: Learn more about the deadly 2026 Ebola outbreak, including how many cases there are so far, which countries are most affected, how this outbreak compares with others in severity and more.

events.title: Russia pressures U.S., Ukraine’s allies to flee Kyiv, threatening ‘systematic strikes’
events.summary: The warning came just days after the Ukrainian capital faced one of the heaviest bombardments since the start of the Russian invasion.

events.title: No people, just sheep: The unlikely job that Chinese college grads clamored for
events.summary: An ad for a shepherding job went viral in China, drawing hundreds of applications from young people eager to escape the stresses of urban life.

events.title: Coast Guard divers arrive in Bahamas to search for missing American Lynette Hooker
events.summary: U.S. Coast Guard divers have arrived in the Bahamas as the agency resumes its search for Lynette Hooker, the American woman who disappeared in April after, her husband said, she fell from a boat, an official said Tuesday

events.title: U.S. plan to open Ebola quarantine center in Kenya faces growing backlash and protests
events.summary: Plans for Americans exposed to Ebola to be quarantined abroad faced mounting backlash, with Kenya’s president defending a proposed 50-bed facility in the country after violent protests.

events.title: Major Russian attack kills at least 22 and leaves Ukraine pleading for help from Trump
events.summary: Russian air attacks on major Ukrainian cities such as Kyiv, Dnipro and Kharkiv killed at least nine and wounded more than 60 early Tuesday, authorities said, after days of warnings that Moscow was planning a major assault.

events.title: Tehran suspends talks with U.S. over Israeli attacks in Lebanon, Iranian media reports
events.summary: Iran suspended talks with the U.S. to protest Israel’s expanding offensive in Lebanon, according to government-aligned media.

events.title: Red stripes in Welsh cave declared Britain’s oldest art after being dismissed as a natural phenomenon
events.summary: Dismissed as a natural phenomenon for more than a century, red stripes on a rock in Wales have been found to be the oldest known prehistoric art in Britain.

events.title: Rescuers race to save last two men trapped in Laos cave
events.summary: With their elbows and knees scraping the walls as they wriggle through claustrophobic tunnels, rescuers are in a race against time to save two men still stuck in a cave in central Laos.

events.title: Brazil and Italy rule out Ebola in previously suspected cases
events.summary: The World Health Organization’s leader traveled to the epicenter of the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo over the weekend, as suspected cases and deaths continue to mount.

events.title: U.S. and Iran trade strikes amid talks to end war
events.summary: The U.S. military said it carried out what it called self-defense strikes on Iranian radar and drone control sites over the weekend, while Iran said it targeted an air base used in the U.S. attack as the two countries continued to trade strikes amid talks to end their war

events.title: French navy seizes Russia-linked oil tanker in high seas, Macron says
events.summary: The French Navy on Sunday boarded an oil tanker, named the Tagor, which was subject to international sanctions and sailing from Russia, French President Emmanuel Macron wrote on X.
